events.label: reasoning
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Subpanel neutral bar is bonded to cabinet with green bonding screw installed, making it a service panel rather than a subpanel — neutral-ground bond not permitted on load side of service disconnect",
    "Neutral and equipment grounding conductors terminate on the same bar in a subpanel, violating separation of grounded and grounding conductors",
    "Two branch-circuit conductors under a single breaker terminal, violating one-conductor-per-terminal rule unless listed for multiple conductors",
    "Unused knockout opening in enclosure lower right side not closed with an approved fitting, compromising enclosure integrity"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove green bonding screw to isolate neutral bar from cabinet",
    "Install separate grounding bar and move all equipment grounding conductors to it; keep only neutral conductors on neutral bar",
    "Separate the two conductors under the single breaker terminal into individual breakers or an approved pigtail/terminal arrangement per listing",
    "Install an approved knockout closure on the unused opening"
  ],
  "rationale": "A subpanel fed by a 4-wire feeder with separate neutral and EGC must not bond the neutral to the enclosure and must keep neutrals and grounds separate. Double-tapping a breaker terminal and leaving an open knockout are additional code violations. These are safety hazards requiring correction before passing rough inspection.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 408 (panelboard construction and wiring)",
    "NEC 408.41 (grounded conductor termination)",
    "NEC 250.6 / 250.24(A)(5) (objectionable current; neutral-ground bond not on load side)",
    "NEC 200.4 (separate neutral and grounding conductor termination)",
    "NEC 110.3(B) (manufacturer listing — one conductor per terminal unless listed)",
    "NEC 110.26 (working clearance — noted clear)"
  ]
}
